title: Malaria Control Strategy Lab
text:
The Global Burden

The Toll: ~249 million cases and 608,000 deaths annually (2022/23 data).
The Target: Reducing incidence by 90% by 2030.
The Metric: \(PfPR_{2-10}\) (Plasmodium falciparum Parasite Rate in children aged 2–10).

This is our “gold standard” for measuring community transmission.

title: Malaria Control Strategy Lab
text:

This site was created to inform others about the research activities and work conducted in the Section on Infectious Diseases as part of our wider social media presence.

title: Malaria Control Strategy Lab
text:
This lab allows you to explore different malaria control strategies given a fixed budget.






🦟


Disease Dynamics Simulator


Run a compartmental malaria model. Adjust transmission, treatment, and vector control parameters to explore how interventions change disease dynamics.










🌿


Vector Environment Lab


Manage larval breeding sites across a landscape. Allocate resources for larviciding and environmental management to reduce mosquito populations.










💊


Pharmacology Lab


Simulate ACT drug resistance and treatment failure. Explore how partner drug combinations and dosing regimens affect parasite clearance.










🔬


Diagnostic Lab


Compare malaria screening strategies. Allocate a budget across RDTs, microscopy, and PCR to optimize detection sensitivity and specificity.










🧬


Vaccine Design Lab


Design a malaria vaccine targeting different parasite life stages. Simulate within-host immune responses and evaluate efficacy trade-offs.
